question_text: Two runners complete the same 400 metre race. Runner A finishes in 50 seconds and Runner B finishes in 55 seconds. Compare the speeds of the two runners and explain which runner is faster.
correct_answer: Runner A is faster. Runner A's speed is 8 m/s (400 ÷ 50) and Runner B's speed is approximately 7.27 m/s (400 ÷ 55). Since 8 m/s > 7.27 m/s, Runner A travels a greater distance per unit time.
explanation: To compare speeds, calculate the speed of each runner using speed = distance ÷ time. Runner A covers the same distance in less time, so has a higher speed. This shows why speed is important in racing—it tells us who is moving fastest.
